meta.title: Dingli Cliffs – Mdina, Malta
meta.description: Complete travel guide to Dingli Cliffs in Malta. Learn about this natural attraction, including location, views, access, and visitor information.

intro.title: Discover Dingli Cliffs
intro.description: Dingli Cliffs are the highest natural point in Malta, with an altitude of approximately 253 meters above sea level. Located on the western part of the island near the village of Dingli, they offer spectacular views of the sea and coastline. This location is famous for its panorama, sunsets, and protected natural areas.

visitGuide.title: Visit Tips
items: The best time to visit is spring or autumn when the weather is pleasant.
During the week at sunset, choose the time before sunset for the best views.
Wear comfortable shoes for walking on natural paths.
Bring water and sun protection especially during summer.

sections.title: Geography & Elevation
sections.content: Dingli Cliffs are located in southwest Malta, with an altitude of approximately 253 meters above sea level. They are the highest point of the island and offer vast views of the Mediterranean Sea.

sections.title: Ecology & Biodiversity
sections.content: The cliffs are part of the Natura 2000 protected area. They host indigenous plants and migratory bird species. This area is important for Malta's biodiversity.

sections.title: Cultural Significance
sections.content: Dingli Cliffs have cultural significance in Maltese mythology. It is said that the island of Filfla, located off the coast, was held by giants in legends of the past.

sections.title: Protection & Conservation
sections.content: The cliffs are managed by the Environment and Resources Authority (ERA) and are listed in the national cultural property inventory (NICPMI). They are protected from excessive development.

hours.title: Visiting Hours
hours.bestTimeSpring: Spring: Pleasant weather, plants in bloom
hours.bestTimeSummer: Summer: Cooler at morning and sunset, avoid midday heat
hours.bestTimeAutumn: Autumn: Mild weather, ideal for walking
hours.bestTimeWinter: Winter: Fewer tourists, pure nature views
hours.tip: There is no entrance fee. It is recommended to visit during the week for stargazing or sunsets.

transport.title: How to Get Here
transport.fromCenterDesc: Distance of about 3km from Mdina. You can reach by bus 201 or 202, or by private car.
transport.fromStationDesc: Line 201 from Valletta/Blaquière or 202 from Sliema/Rabat.
transport.fromAirportDesc: Distance of about 12km. It is recommended to take a taxi or rental car to Dingli.
transport.publicTransportDesc: Lines 201 and 202 stop near Dingli Village.
transport.walkingDesc: Walk from Dingli Village to the cliffs in a few minutes.
transport.drivingDesc: Parking available near the cliff area.

route.title: Recommended Route
route.overview: Walk along the cliffs and enjoy the natural views.
steps: Start from the parking near Dingli Village center
Walk along the main path along the cliffs
Reach the point with the best view of the Mediterranean Sea

spots.title: Main Viewpoint
spots.description: The place where you can take the most classic photos of the cliffs and the sea.

spots.title: Dingli Sunset Point
spots.description: Ideal location for stargazing and nature quietness.

spots.title: Panorama from Heights
spots.description: The cliff slopes and the blue sea in the distance.

reviews.title: Visitor Reviews
reviews.declaration: Reviews can be viewed via Google Maps.
reviews.moreReviews: View more reviews on Google Maps

items.name: John C.
items.text: Incredible place! The highest point in Malta with spectacular views. I recommend visiting at sunset.

items.name: Mary G.
items.text: Went with my parents and it was excellent. Calm and relaxing place, perfect for family visit.

items.name: David P.
items.text: Very beautiful place for walking. The views are superb. I only had a bit of difficulty finding parking on the weekend.

items.name: Helen S.
items.text: For a visit to Malta, this is one of the top natural attractions you shouldn't miss. The trees and sea are very romantic.

items.name: Mark A.
items.text: Was here for stargazing and it was a unique experience. No city noise, only the sky and sea.

items.name: Claire B.
items.text: Perfect place for a weekend picnic. Get food nearby and enjoy the view.

officialManagement.title: About Dingli Cliffs
officialManagement.text: Dingli Cliffs are managed and protected by the Environment and Resources Authority (ERA) of Malta. The area is listed as a protected site in the national catalog and Natura 2000 list.
